acceptanceSignals: All official external HTTP endpoints are present in OpenAPI 3.1.
CI publishes machine-readable spec and browsable API docs artifacts.
Generated client/types build without manual type patching.

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: OpenAPI Contract Platform in 0 attempts

reinforcementResults.name: No Legacy Path Rule
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass based on workspace evidence: the code defines a canonical OpenAPI 3.1 contract for employee/vendor/admin/integration APIs, includes explicit schemas/errors/auth/pagination-sort-filter/enums/validation constraints, enforces operation/auth-model consistency in tests, and CI exports machine-readable spec plus browsable docs artifacts while validating generated TypeScript client/types via contract generation and compile checks. No legacy/fallback compatibility path evidence was found in current source/tests for this feature scope.

excludedApproaches: Shipping externally used endpoints that are absent from OpenAPI.
Treating handwritten API interfaces as primary contract source.
Deferring contract updates to later releases.
executionConstraints: OpenAPI 3.1 is the formal contract for employee, vendor, admin, and integration HTTP APIs.
Contract must define schemas, errors, auth, pagination/sort/filter, enums, and validation constraints.
Auth declarations in the spec must match the locked SSO+vendor-MFA model.
name: OpenAPI Contract Platform
rationale: Contract-first control is required to prevent implementation and integration drift.
summary: Build and enforce the canonical machine-verifiable HTTP contract.
